issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.9:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .features-section .section-header p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff). At 18px font size (large text), this requires a 3:1 contrast ratio, but 2.9:1 is below this threshold. Users with low vision will have difficulty reading this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1008: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, and no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline is provided as a replacement. Keyboard users will not see any focus indicator on focused elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible alternative (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline) is provided in this rule or the base .btn rule. Keyboard users will not see a focus indicator on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and removes the clipping that hides it visually. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only behavior and may cause layout issues while exposing content to all users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn and .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 564-569), and .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px) (line 582). This spatial movement animation is not disabled inside the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (line 416), which is empty. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from this animation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px (below 44px) with padding: 0. This creates a 28×28 px touch target, below the minimum 24×24 px and far below the recommended 44×44 px, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px (below the 44px recommended minimum) with vertical padding of only 0.375rem (4.5px at the 12px root font size), which is less than the 8px padding needed to compensate. This creates a touch target that may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to select this small target.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended target size. This icon button is difficult to activate for users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is set to width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.5). This makes the control inaccessible for many touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which falls in the 24–43px range. Without compensating padding (none is defined in this rule), the effective touch target is likely below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which renders content fully visible on screen. This breaks the intended "screen reader only" functionality, exposing helper text to all users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disabling this specific transition, which may cause vestibular discomfort for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties (Line 560) and uses transform: translateY(1px) on active state. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this spatial movement for the base button component.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector defines a transition including transform and uses transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. This motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, which fails the 3:1 minimum for large text (18px+).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The card body text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (14px).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline)
issuesFound.explanation: The product description text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The plan tagline text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline entirely. In Windows High Contrast Mode, this leaves users with no focus indicator at all, as box-shadow is also suppressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360-362: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This makes it impossible for sighted keyboard users to determine which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 349: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to appear merged.
issuesFound.fix: Line 117: word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector suppresses the default outline without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from anyone. Screen reader only content must be clipped or positioned off-screen while remaining in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378-387: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without replacement. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so users receive no focus indication at all.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or a non-zero outline. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users on all interactive elements, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 343: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, resulting in a touch target at the absolute minimum that may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1171: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class sets outline-offset but does not restore a visible outline style after the global outline: none, leaving focus invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 361: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 371: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28px touch target that is below the recommended 44px and lacks compensating spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 616: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards include transform transitions for vertical movement and scaling on hover, with no reduced-motion override to disable these animations.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 920: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by making words run together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: Primary buttons use background-color #5588bb with white text, creating a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (font-weight 600 at 16px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 430: `.btn-primary { background-color: #004aaa; border-color: #004aaa; color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: Danger buttons use background-color #ee9999 with white text, creating a contrast ratio of approximately 1.48:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 450: `.btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .required-marker
issuesFound.explanation: Required field markers use color #ee9999 against white backgrounds, creating a contrast ratio of approximately 1.48:1, which fails the 3:1 requirement for large text (bold 16px) and 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 398: `.required-marker { color: var(--colour-error-dark); margin-left: var(--space-0-5); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); font-size: 1em; line-height: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button base class includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on interaction, but has no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) — Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes transform in its transition, creating spatial movement on hover, but has no prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 837: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) — Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link includes transform in its transition, creating spatial movement when revealed, but has no prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 406: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) — Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } } }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, and assistive technologies cannot convey the error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the `markInvalid` function, before the existing `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` line.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will still announce the button as "collapsed" even though the menu is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` inside the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the function emits a custom event but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users who cannot see the screen will not be notified that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });` inside the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it never sets the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` inside the `_openInstance` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content, but no announcement is made to screen readers. While focus moves to the first item for keyboard users, mouse users and those using screen readers without keyboard navigation will not be notified that content has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` immediately before the line `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });` inside the `_openInstance` function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the function updates the open state and animates the panel height, but it never sets the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` inside the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed, the function hides the panel and updates aria-expanded, but does not announce the closure via a live region. Users may not realize the menu has disappeared, especially if focus is not explicitly returned to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` immediately before the line `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });` inside the `_closeInstance` function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close function updates aria-expanded and hides the panel but does not announce the collapse action to screen readers. While the open function announces the expansion, the lack of a corresponding announcement for closing creates an inconsistent experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` immediately before the line `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });` inside the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never created or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap()` in the `init` function, nor activates it in the `open` function. When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay, and focus is not returned to the trigger button on close.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In the `init` function, after `_initialised = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`. In the `open` function, after `_lockScroll();`, add `_trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as a toggle group where one is always active, but the function only updates CSS classes and never sets the aria-pressed attribute. Screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently active because the pressed state is not reflected in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside the `forEach` loop in `_syncTabAriaStates`, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds the open class to the drawer and announces the state change, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetric state management that leaves screen readers unaware the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance() function sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to incorrectly report the menu state as collapsed when it is visually open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function removes the hidden attribute from the answer panel and announces the expansion, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close() function sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in incorrect state reporting for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from explicitly announcing the field is invalid, relying solely on the description which may be missed or misinterpreted.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() function makes the menu panel visible but does not write to an aria-live region. Unlike mobileNav and faqAccordion which announce their state changes, dropdown menus open silently, depriving screen reader users of confirmation that the menu is available.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance() function hides the menu panel but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu closes, which can cause confusion about the current interface state.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _showBanner function does not announce banner appearance
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner() function displays the consent dialog and traps focus but does not announce the banner's appearance via a live region. Users may not understand why focus has suddenly moved or what the new context is.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true". Consequently, screen readers do not announce the field as invalid when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1034: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null checks and before the `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module reveals the navigation drawer and locks scroll, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1148: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment and before the classList operations.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function displays the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the panel's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` and before `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel height and updates internal state, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the item's trigger button. Screen readers cannot convey that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2936: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When closing an FAQ item, the close() function animates the panel closed and updates state, but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The attribute remains stale, causing screen readers to incorrectly report the panel as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2966: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `item.open = false;` and before `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() does not update aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles visual active states for category filter buttons but never updates the aria-pressed attribute. Because these buttons act as toggle buttons (aria-pressed is expected per the HTML contract comment), screen readers cannot convey which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2676: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop, immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds visual classes to open the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers announce the menu as closed even when it is visually open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds visual classes but does not update the trigger's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The corresponding _closeInstance function sets it to "false", causing screen readers to misidentify the dropdown state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel visually but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", leading to incorrect accessibility tree state for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. The markValid function removes aria-invalid, creating an asymmetry where invalid fields are not programmatically identified as invalid.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open and close actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions change visible content state but do not announce the change via a live region. Users relying on screen readers are not informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes automatically or via interaction.

Consequently, all forms using the inline validator (contactForm, newsletterForm, multiStepWizard) fail to communicate the invalid state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded="true" on trigger

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true

The `close()` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set the initial `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. If the menu is closed on load, the trigger should explicitly have `aria-expanded="false"`.

fpTitles: mobileNav missing focus management on open
mobileNav missing focus return on close
dropdownMenu focus not returned on outside close
productFilter changes not announced
mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded
dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not update aria-expanded
faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded

scrollToTop action not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function marks the field with CSS classes and links error messages via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (inside _renderResult when result.valid is false, after addClass): setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
  Line (inside _renderResult when result.valid is true, after removeClass): setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions update CSS classes and emit events, but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the state change. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open() after _lockScroll()): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line (in close() after _unlockScroll()): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters, the function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but does not announce the result count change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology are not informed how many products match their selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches) in applyFilters): liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller shows and hides panels using CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but fails to update aria-expanded on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing when a dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance after addClass): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line (in _closeInstance after removeClass): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox with a suggestion listbox. When the listbox opens and closes, the code updates internal state but never toggles aria-expanded on the input element, leaving screen reader users unaware of the listbox visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _open after _searchState.open = true): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line (in _close after _searchState.open = false): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function activates a focus trap but does not move keyboard focus into the drawer. Focus remains on the trigger button, forcing screen reader users to manually navigate to the newly opened content.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes and returnFocus is requested, the function does not return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _closeInstance after emit, inside if (returnFocus)): inst.trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created in the DOM with no aria-expanded attribute. The init function never sets aria-expanded="false" to indicate the closed initial state, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial DOM state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after _initialised = true): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but never initialises aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to match the initially hidden panel state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in register() after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with a listbox, but the init function never sets aria-expanded="false" to indicate the initially closed state of the suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl)): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button controls the drawer element (_drawer), but the init function never sets aria-controls to programmatically associate the trigger with the drawer region it expands.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when interacting with the toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1315: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1345: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button (_trigger). Assistive technologies will not know the initial closed state of the menu until the first interaction occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1415: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") via the liveRegion module. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1315: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
  Line 1345: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not initialised on registration
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions do not announce the dropdown state change via the liveRegion module. Users are not notified when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 1565: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function sets the item state and shows the panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2315: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's _syncToggleState() function toggles the CSS active class on billing period buttons (_periodBtns) but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Screen reader users cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2735: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing setPeriod() function updates the UI and emits an event but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") via the liveRegion module.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700: liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _updateCountBadge() function updates the text and aria-label of the count badge but does not announce the new count via a live region. Users are not informed when the number of visible products changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2095: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function, used by inlineValidator, links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4950: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` inside the markInvalid function after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instances missing aria-expanded state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not toggle aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot track whether the dropdowns are open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6150: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside _openInstance.
  Line 6170: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions emit custom events but do not announce the navigation state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5825: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` before the emit call in open().
  Line 5850: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the emit call in close().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel is shown and hidden via _open and _close functions, but the combobox input element never receives an aria-expanded attribute to indicate the popup state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 8050: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;` inside _open.
  Line 8070: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;` inside _close.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus updates the visual state but does not announce the change via the live region. Screen reader users are unaware when a submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6155: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` before emit in _openInstance.
  Line 6175: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before emit in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices switched to monthly or annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 9350: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after `_syncToggleState(period);` in setPeriod.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When mobileNav initializes, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers encounter the button without knowing it controls a collapsed menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5800: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_initialised = true;` in the init function (following the event listener attachments).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ items updates the DOM but does not announce the state change. Screen reader users cannot tell whether an answer is now open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10200: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened ? STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) : '${item.question} expanded');` before emit in open().
  Line 10250: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed ? STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) : '${item.question} collapsed');` before emit in close().

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility removes the error description but does not reset aria-invalid to false or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4970: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` inside the markValid function after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (showing one active category at a time) but _syncTabAriaStates only toggles CSS classes. The buttons lack aria-pressed to communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 8750: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register function prepares dropdown triggers but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on them. The attribute is absent until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6120: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` in the register function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount function sets up the combobox pattern but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the input element. The popup state is unknown to screen readers on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7980: Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` in the mount function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens as a modal-like overlay but does not trap keyboard focus. Users can tab out of the drawer and into the background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open`, `mobileNav.close`, and `mobileNav.toggle` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions update the UI but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to the `liveRegion`. Screen reader users are not notified of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1370: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);
  Line 1385: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions show or hide the panel but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `inst.trigger` button. Screen reader users will not know if the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.register` function sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. The initial state is undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not announce state change to live region

Line 1530: liveRegion.announce(`Dropdown ${inst.trigger.textContent} opened`);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function sets `item.open = true` and removes `hidden` but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on `item.trigger`. The `close` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `faqAccordion.close` functions update the accordion state but do not announce the change (e.g., "answer expanded") to the `liveRegion`. Screen reader users are not notified of the interaction result.

issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function adds `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid` function removes `aria-describedby` references but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field. The invalid state may persist for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading": `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The section contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby, leaving the landmark region without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading": `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements, but the MobileNav internal nav lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Mobile": `<nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup error message not rendered due to prop mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup expects errorMessage prop but receives error prop, causing validation error messages not to render, preventing users from knowing what correction is needed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change prop from `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` and add `errorId="newsletter-email-error"`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup error messages not rendered due to prop mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup expects errorMessage prop but receives error prop, causing validation error messages not to render for any field.
issuesFound.fix: Line: For each FormGroup, change `error={...}` to `errorMessage={...}` and add appropriate `errorId` prop (e.g., errorId="contact-name-error")

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input has aria-describedby pointing to "newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id exists in the DOM because FormGroup was not passed the errorId prop, causing screen readers to not associate error messages with the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Ensure FormGroup receives errorId="newsletter-email-error" and renders ErrorMessage with that id, or remove aria-describedby until error messaging is properly implemented.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea has aria-describedby including "contact-message-error", but no element with that id exists because FormGroup was not passed the errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup and ensure it renders ErrorMessage with that id.

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute misplaced
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog", which may cause screen readers to incorrectly handle the modal's modal behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Move aria-modal="true" from overlay div to the dialog div: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel uses role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", which is needed to indicate that the dialog is modal and content outside should be inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-modal="true": `<div role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={...} ...>`

issuesFound.title: Skip links navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Since there are multiple <nav> landmarks on the page (main, mobile, footer), this landmark must be distinguished to allow screen reader users to navigate directly to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 357: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not pass aria-busy={isLoading} to the underlying <button> element. Assistive technology users are not informed when the button is in a busy/loading state.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card uses an article element containing a heading, but lacks aria-labelledby referencing that heading. Screen reader users cannot identify which plan the card represents when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1102: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating component contains Unicode star characters that are not hidden, causing screen readers to announce them individually despite the container having an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 254-256: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator uses an asterisk character that is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" instead of conveying the required status semantically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: *

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span inside the Button component is decorative but lacks aria-hidden, causing redundant announcements alongside the loadingLabel text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 298: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: TextInput component ignores invalid prop for ARIA state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid="true" when invalid is true. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, confusing users during form validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component ignores invalid prop for ARIA state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid="true" when invalid is true. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, confusing users during form validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Section component drops labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but fails to render it as aria-labelledby on the underlying element. This breaks the accessible name relationship for sections using this component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 655: <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>{children}</Tag>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element wrapping the skip links lacks an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav landmarks on the page (header, mobile, footer), this nav is indistinguishable to screen reader users navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron span (▲/▼) inside the dropdown trigger contains text characters but is purely decorative. It should be hidden from assistive technology to prevent noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 740: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation nav element lacks an aria-label. With multiple nav landmarks on the page, this region is indistinguishable to screen reader users navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page (via item.current) uses a visual class but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed which link represents the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 763: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing accessible label for formatted text
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic value displays formatted text (e.g., "40,000+") but does not use the provided stat.valueLabel (e.g., "40,000 plus") for aria-label. Screen readers may pronounce symbols incorrectly or ambiguously.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 935:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar decorative clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second ul in the integrations marquee is a visual duplicate used for looping animation but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users will hear the list of integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1037: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">
